title: Preparation method of pyridylaminated N-glycans
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"7hied","text":"This protocol describes the purification of N-glycans from approximately 50 µg of protein samples, followed by fluorescent labeling with 2-aminopyridine. The procedure utilizes Rapid PNGase F (New England BioLabs) and BlotGlyco (Sumitomo Bakelite). Representative examples include the preparation of human serum, human urine, and CHO-K1 membrane fractions; however, the protocol is also applicable to other types of samples.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":44,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: 50-100 µg glycoprotein (dry sample) in 0.2 mL PCR tube

RP-LC/MS analysis LC/MS condition: Hanzawa K, Suzuki N, Natsuka S. Structures and developmental alterations of N-glycans of zebrafish embryos. Glycobiology , 2017;27(3):228–245. DOI: 10.1093/glycob/cww124
dissolved in 100 µL of DDW
lyophilize
heat at 70 °C for 00:30:00

transfer collected solution to 0.6 mL PCR tube with 200 µL of 0.28% ammonium aq. (2.8% ammonium aq.: 200 µL of 28% ammonium aq., 1.8 mL of DDW) (0.28% ammonium aq.: 100 µL of 2.8% ammonium aq., 900 µL of DDW) A step to remove O -acetylated by-products
2000 rpm, 25 °C, 1 minute and 13200 rpm, 25 °C, 30 seconds
add 25 µL of 0.1% acetic acid (1% acetic acid: 10 µL of 100% acetic acid, 990 µL of DDW) (0.1% acetic acid: 10 µL of 1% acetic acid, 90 µL of DDW)
add 25 µL of DDW
replace the reaction tube into a new 1.5 mL tube
wash with 600 µL of 95%(v/v) acetonitrile, 2000 rpm, 25 °C, 1 minute and 13200 rpm, 25 °C, 30 seconds
wash with 600 µL of 95%(v/v) acetonitrile, cfg. 2000 rpm, 25 °C, 1 minute and 13200 rpm, 25 °C, 30 seconds ( 1.9 mL of CH 3 CN, 100 µL of DDW)

2000 rpm, 25 °C, 1 minute and 13200 rpm, 25 °C, 30 seconds Modification from manufacturer's protocol: apply the solution to the column by gentle centrifugation, then spin briefly at high speed to pass the liquid through. Perform the following centrifugation steps in the same manner.
wlet the fluid drop until completely loaded Modification from manufacturer's protocol: wait until all the liquid has been loaded onto the column.
washed with 200 µL of acetonitrile, twice
washed with 200 µL of DDW
add the sample aliquot to the cleanup column in a centrifuge
add 1080 µL of acetonitrile to prepare 90% acetonitrile sample aliquot Modification from manufacturer's protocol: prepare 90% acetonitrile sample aliquot, considering the volatility of acetic acid.
13.200 rpm, 25 °C, 1 minute
replace the reaction tube into a new 1.5 mL tube
heat at 80°C for 35 min
add 110 µL of reduction reagent , mix by pipetting ( 100 mg of Me 2 NH-borane, 40 µL of acetic acidtic acid, 25 µL of DDW) Modification from manufacturer's protocol: mix by pipetting.
heat at 80 °C for 01:00:00
add 30 µL PAtion reagent ( 138 mg of 2-aminopyridine, 50 µL of acetic acid)
heat at 70 °C for 01:30:00 , without cover (the solvent has completely evaporated.) (If the beads are not dry completely, continue heating for another 00:15:00
add 180 µL of 2% acetic acid in acetonitrile, mix by pipetting ( 100 µL of acetic acid, 4.9 mL of acetonitrile) Modification from manufacturer's protocol: mix by pipetting.
add 20 µL of DDW
replace the reaction tube into the 1.5 mL tube
wash with 200 µL of DDW, twice
spin down to remove the fluid
replace the reaction tube into the 2 mL tube
leave at room temperature for 00:30:00
add 100 µL of 1% triethylamine in MeOH, mix by pipetting ( 15 µL of acetic anhydride, 135 µL of MeOH) Modification from manufacturer's protocol: mix by pipetting.
replace the reaction tube into the 1.5 mL tube
wash with 200 µL of 1% triethylamine in MeOH
wash with 200 µL of 1% triethylamine in MeOH, mix by pipetting ( 100 µL of triethylamine, 9.9 mL of MeOH) Modification from manufacturer's protocol: mix by pipetting.
wash with 200 µL of DDW
wash with 200 µL of DDW, mix by pipetting Modification from manufacturer's protocol: mix by pipetting.
wash with 200 µL of 2M guanidine solution ( 1.9 g guanidine hydrochloride, 10 mL of DDW)
replace the reaction tube into the 2 mL tube
heat at 80 °C for 01:00:00 , without cover (the solvent has completely evaporated.) (If the beads are not dry completely, continue heating for another 00:15:00 )
add sample solution to the reaction tube, mix by pipetting Modification from manufacturer's protocol: mix by pipetting.
add 180 µL of 2% acetic acid in acetonitrile to the reaction tub 20 µL e ( 20 µL of 980 µL 980 µL a 980 µL c 980 µL etic 980 µL aci 980 µL d of acetonitrile)
replace the reaction tube into a new 1.5 mL tube
wash polymer beads with 100 µL of DDW, twice, spin down to remove water completely Modification from manufacturer's protocol: wash polymer beads with 100 µL of DDW, twice
50 µL of polymer beads suspension in the reaction tube BlotGlyco Sumitomo Bakelite Catalog #BS-45414
incubate at 50 °C for 00:10:00
add 1 µL of Rapid PNGase F, mix by pipetting
cool down the sample solution
denature at 80 °C for 00:02:00
add 4 µL of Rapid PNGase F Buffer (5x) to sample solution, mix gently with a pipette—total reaction volume to 20 µL Rapid PNGase F New England Biolabs Catalog #P0710S
transfer 16 µL (protein content 50-100 µg) to 0.2 mL PCR tube
vortex
add XX µL of 0.1% NP-40 ( 10 µL of 10% NP-40, 990 µL of DDW)
1 mg of CHO-K1 cell membrane fraction (dry sample) in 0.2 mL PCR tube
vortex
add 16 µL of DDW
50 µg of human urinary protein ppt. (dry sample) in 0.2 mL PCR tube
vortex
add 15 µL of DDW
1 µL of human serum in 0.2 mL PCR tube
vortex
add 15 µL of DDW
2000 rpm, 25 °C, 1 minute and 13200 rpm, 25 °C, 30 seconds
